our_argument_summary: Verdict Std Model Explains. WIN-043 claims a 2.26× longitudinal-to-latitudinal NMP drift ratio. Our review argues the value is post-hoc retrodiction from public NOAA NP.xy data, has no derivation from the dome's axially-symmetric H(r) field equation, and is fully explained by standard core-mantle boundary flux-lobe dynamics (Livermore et al. 2020). The c3 review added a post-hoc-test paragraph (per the dome's own definition) and identified two unaddressed holes: a major missing argument (W019 self-contradiction — dome's own weekly test was REFINED with a 5.5× lateral:poleward ratio) and a moderate missing cross-reference to the Axial Symmetry Paradox section. Both holes target detail_extra, which is null. This c4 verifies whether those c3 holes still apply on current content.
re_review_intent: queue_id 314 dispatched 2026-05-09T08:50:00.790Z requesting verification of c3 holes against current data/wins.json. This c4 confirms persistence; it is intentionally lightweight (verification, not a fresh adversarial pass).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3: monitor.py is not in the current raw-text snapshot; per the C1/C2 chain, Domain #11 fetches NOAA NP.xy and only checks current longitude > 130°E. No domain decomposes drift into latitudinal/longitudinal velocity components. The 2.26× ratio is unmonitored homepage text.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — there is no monitoring code for this specific ratio. The dome's W019 weekly test asks a related but distinct question (does poleward drift dominate lateral) and the dome itself marked it REFINED, conceding lateral drift dominates 5.5×. WIN-043 (CONFIRMED, ratio=2.26×) and W019 (REFINED, dome-reported ratio=5.5×) report disagreeing ratios for the same physical asymmetry.
data_sources_used: NOAA NGDC NP.xy pole-position time series (1590-2025; same dataset as WIN-006/007/036)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged: the 2.26× value lacks methodological specification (no time window, no velocity method, no coordinate system, no unit basis). c3 verified the value IS approximately reproducible in km/yr units over 1990-2025 (2.16×) — but recoverable-from-public-data is the definition of post-hoc retrodiction, not a defense.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: No discrepancies. Our review does not over-claim what the dome's monitoring does.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c3. No automation computes velocity-component ratios for this WIN. Domain #11 only validates longitude > 130°E threshold per the C1/C2 chain. The 2.26× value is unmonitored homepage text.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Livermore et al. 2020 (Nature Geoscience, doi:10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9) attributes predominantly longitudinal drift to flux-lobe competition at the core-mantle boundary. The dome renames the cause but provides no alternative numerical derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. NOAA NP.xy data publicly indexed back to 1590; the c3 detail_verdict_text rewrite tied this to Part 3.1b's legitimate-novelty carve-out. PRED-R004 (≥2.0× through 2028) is recycled from the same retrodiction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. The dome's H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) is axially symmetric. No formula derives 2.26× from disc_radius, firmament_height, or the n(r) refractive profile.

holes_found.description: PERSISTING from c3 (also tracked in ISS-1902, status:open as of this run's check). The c3 major hole — that WIN-043 text does not reference the dome's self-contradiction with W019 — remains unaddressed. detail_extra is still null. Confirmed via fresh clone + git pull --rebase: zero textual change to WIN-043 since c3 was written 2026-05-09T07:06:25Z. The dome registered W019 ('poleward NMP drift dominates lateral') and then marked it REFINED upon observing 5.5× larger lateral drift — a value 2.4× higher than WIN-043's 2.26× CONFIRMED ratio. Until the analyst writes the detail_extra paragraph specified in c3 hole #1 (or its equivalent), this is a live missing-argument hole.
holes_found.recommendation: No new recommendation — the c3 wording template stands. The decider should keep ISS-1902 open and prod the analyst. Once detail_extra is populated with the W019 self-contradiction paragraph, queue WIN-043 for c5 verification.

holes_found.recommendation: No new recommendation — the c3 wording template stands. Decider should keep ISS-1903 open. Both holes can be fixed in the same detail_extra paragraph.

stronger_arguments.description: No new strengthening arguments beyond c3. The c3 list (W019 falsification narrative; PRED-R004 forward-looking falsification window) remains the priority. Worth noting: the W019 verdict_history shows the falsified flip was even silently reverted once before being restored by the curmudgeon (2026-04-20 commit 209fda5 → restore) — the pattern of dome-self-contradiction has additional teeth from the silent-revert audit trail.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c3 (data/wins.json:WIN-043.detail_evidence). The directional asymmetry of NMP drift is real. NOAA NP.xy data confirms longitudinal velocity has dominated latitudinal velocity for the post-1990 trajectory; computed in km/yr over 1990-2025 the ratio is 2.16× — within 5% of the dome's claimed 2.26× (verified by c3 quantitative_verification, not re-run this cycle).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Unchanged from c3. Three independent failures persist: (1) post-hoc retrodiction from indexed public data (data/wins.json:WIN-043.detail_verdict_text already documents this via Part 3.1b cross-reference); (2) no dome derivation possible because H(r) is axially symmetric (data/wins.json:WIN-043.detail_evidence acknowledges the symmetry implicitly; sections.json Axial Symmetry Paradox section names WIN-043 explicitly — but WIN-043 itself does not yet link there); (3) self-inconsistency with dome's own W019 (data/predictions.json:W019.notes reports 5.5× lateral:poleward, contradicting WIN-043's 2.26× by a factor of 2.4×).

advocate_mode.best_defense: Speaking as the dome defender (carryover from c3, abbreviated — verification cycle, no fresh steelman regenerated): the verifiability point is partially correct (we showed it ourselves in c3 quantitative_verification: km/yr 1990-2025 ratio is 2.16×, within 5% of claim). Reproducibility-from-public-data is acknowledged. The defense survives 3/5 because the c3 detail_verdict_text already preempts via Part 3.1b. The W019 self-contradiction (still missing in current text) would dissolve the remaining steelman.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Same as c3: integrate the W019 self-contradiction (Hole #1) and the Axial Symmetry Paradox cross-reference (Hole #2). Both close the lingering steelman.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No internal review-side inconsistencies (carryover from c3). All four NOAA-NP.xy-derived WINs (006, 007, 036, 043) are consistently classified as post-hoc retrodictions with no dome-geometry derivation, all citing axial symmetry as the structural problem.

quantitative_verification.discrepancies: No NEW quantitative claim was added or modified since c3, so c3's NOAA NP.xy ratio computations (1990-2025: 2.16×; 2020-2025: 2.34×; etc.) carry forward unchanged. Not re-run this cycle to avoid burning the per-cycle Lens C budget on a no-op.

deeper_analysis:
C4 verification review of WIN-043, dispatched via priority-queue queue_id 314 (target_type:win-detail-rewrite, reason cites ISS-1902 W019 missing argument and ISS-1903 axial-symmetry cross-ref missing, reason note 'detail_extra null, needs analyst content addition'). The premise of the queue dispatch — that detail_extra needs analyst content — is correct: detail_extra is still null and zero textual change has occurred to WIN-043 since c3 was written 2026-05-09T07:06:25Z (before the queue_id 314 push at 08:50:00Z). Both c3 holes are unmodified live holes against current content. ISS-1902 and ISS-1903 remain open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json (verified this cycle).

Because this c4 is a verification cycle (no new content to attack, no new strengthenings to discover), the per-cycle review lenses (Lens A advocate-mode, Lens B cross-WIN consistency, Lens C quantitative-verification) carry forward c3 outputs without re-derivation. This is the appropriate posture for a verification-only c4 — exhaustive re-derivation when content has not changed dilutes adversarial focus and burns Opus budget on a no-op. If the analyst patches detail_extra and curmudgeon is requeued for c5, the lenses should be re-run fresh on the patched content.

Decider action: the queue_id 314 entry can be popped from priority-queue.json now that c4 with queue_id=314 is on disk. Keep ISS-1902 (major) and ISS-1903 (moderate) open. Prod the analyst for detail_extra content via the standard channel (analyst-tracker or human-note). Once detail_extra is non-null, requeue with target_type:win-detail-rewrite for c5 verification.

Other-side consideration: a defender of skipping this c4 entirely (Step 5 superseded-findings exception spirit) would argue 'no content changed, no new finding, no review file needed.' But Step 5's skip-without-writing path is for findings that have been patched; here findings are persisting, not superseded. The decider explicitly dispatched queue_id 314 wanting a verification signal, and without queue_id=314 on disk the decider's pop filter cannot retire the queue entry. Writing a thin verification-c4 is the lower-friction path, even if the per-cycle lens budget is largely amortized from c3.

Recommended action remains minor_edit (analyst writes detail_extra). Confidence 0.94. Verdict Std Model Explains holds (unchanged from c1/c2/c3).
